title: Section 8 Apartments for Rent in Maple Heights, OH
metaTitle: Section 8 Apartments for Rent in Maple Heights, OH
metaDescription: Section 8 apartments for rent in Maple Heights, OH: how the CMHA voucher process works step by step, and the nearest voucher-friendly Cleveland homes.
primaryKeyword: section 8 apartments for rent Maple Heights ohio
secondaryKeywords: voucher friendly rentals near buckeye shaker
answerBox: Maple Heights is a Cuyahoga County suburb southeast of Cleveland, bordering Garfield Heights, Bedford, and Warrensville Heights. CMHA administers Housing Choice Vouchers for the whole county through a year-round online application and random selection. We don't yet manage units inside Maple Heights, but work with voucher-friendly rental homes nearby in Buckeye-Shaker and Lee-Harvard.

sections.h2: Are there Section 8 apartments for rent in Maple Heights?
body: Some are, since accepting a Housing Choice Voucher is generally each landlord's own choice in Maple Heights rather than a legal requirement. Maple Heights sits southeast of Cleveland, bordering Garfield Heights, Bedford, and Warrensville Heights. Rent Finder Cleveland doesn't currently work with apartments inside Maple Heights itself, but our closest managed homes are in **Buckeye-Shaker** (ZIP 44120), a corridor that runs along the RTA Blue and Green light-rail lines, and in the **Lee-Harvard** area (ZIP 44128) a bit farther south.
[Book a showing](/p/book-showing) to see current availability in those neighborhoods, or let our leasing team know if a Maple Heights address is what you're after — we'll flag you if that changes.

sections.h2: The Section 8 voucher process, step by step
body: Every Housing Choice Voucher used in or near Maple Heights runs through [CMHA](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php), the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority, which administers the program countywide. The process has a few distinct stages, and knowing the order helps set realistic expectations about timing.

rows: CMHA verifies household income and information
Selection is a random "mini-lottery," not first-come-first-served
Look for a landlord willing to accept the voucher
Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers
The specific unit must pass a housing-quality inspection
HUD is transitioning from HQS to NSPIRE; final deadline Feb. 1, 2027
Lease begins; household income is recertified annually
Report household changes to CMHA promptly

sections.h2: Does Maple Heights have its own voucher protections?
body: Not that current reporting shows. Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law, so protection against voucher discrimination depends on whether a specific city has passed its own ordinance. Reporting identifies Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, and Warrensville Heights as having done so; Maple Heights is not among them, and neither is the City of Cleveland itself. In practice, that means a Maple Heights landlord can lawfully decline an applicant for using a voucher, even though the voucher itself is valid anywhere within CMHA's jurisdiction.

See our [source-of-income discrimination guide](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/source-of-income-discrimination-cleveland-ohio/) for the full jurisdiction-by-jurisdiction picture across Cuyahoga County, and confirm any specific listing's policy directly before applying.

Maple Heights has no rail station of its own, but it borders the Buckeye-Shaker corridor, where the RTA Blue and Green light-rail lines run along Shaker Boulevard toward Shaker Square and Downtown's Tower City. [RTA's regular bus network](https://www.riderta.com/routes) also serves Maple Heights directly, connecting it to both light-rail stations and the wider county system.

sections.h2: Nearest Section 8-friendly homes we work with
body: Rent Finder Cleveland's 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes are concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side, and the closest to Maple Heights are in Buckeye-Shaker and, to a lesser extent, Lee-Harvard. Every home we work with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is kept HUD-inspection-ready.

[Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see what's currently available near Maple Heights, or read our [income-limit guide](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/section-8-income-limits-cuyahoga-county/) to check whether your household qualifies before you apply to CMHA.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with Section 8 apartments in Maple Heights?
faq.a: Not currently. Our closest voucher-friendly rental homes sit in Buckeye-Shaker (44120) and, to a lesser extent, Lee-Harvard (44128), both bordering Maple Heights. Every home we work with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Tell our team what you need and we'll point you to the nearest fit.

faq.q: How long does it take to get a Section 8 voucher near Maple Heights?
faq.a: CMHA's preliminary application is open year-round, but selection happens through random mini-lotteries rather than a strict queue, so being reached can take anywhere from a few days to several months. There's no fixed timeline CMHA publishes for an individual applicant.

faq.q: Does Maple Heights protect Section 8 voucher holders from discrimination?
faq.a: Reporting does not list Maple Heights among the Cuyahoga County suburbs with their own source-of-income protection ordinance, unlike Cleveland Heights and Warrensville Heights. Ohio has no statewide law on this either, so acceptance in Maple Heights is generally up to each landlord.

faq.q: What has to happen before a voucher can be used on a specific unit?
faq.a: The unit must pass a housing-quality inspection administered through CMHA. HUD is currently transitioning that inspection standard from the older HQS framework to NSPIRE, with a final deadline of February 1, 2027 for the Housing Choice Voucher program.

faq.q: What's the closest Section 8-friendly rental to Maple Heights?
faq.a: Buckeye-Shaker (ZIP 44120) is our closest concentration of voucher-friendly homes to Maple Heights, along the RTA Blue and Green light-rail corridor, with additional homes farther south in the Lee-Harvard area (ZIP 44128).
